text.title: Rust Testing Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules covering layer coverage, the cargo-llvm-cov gate, doctest discipline, proptest scope, and integration-tests layout.

rule.statement: Every published crate has unit (`#[cfg(test)] mod tests`), integration (`tests/*.rs`), AND doctest (`///` examples) layers. CI runs all three: `cargo test --lib && cargo test --tests && cargo test --doc`.
rule.rationale: Each layer catches a different class of bug. Unit covers internal invariants. Integration verifies the public API like a downstream consumer. Doctests guarantee documentation does not drift from code.

rule.statement: Use `cargo-llvm-cov` for branch coverage. CI gate fails PR if diff-cover < 85% on changed lines. Repo-wide threshold is reported but not blocking (per [[code-coverage]]).
rule.rationale: Tarpaulin is line-only and slower; llvm-cov measures branch + line via the LLVM source-coverage infrastructure. Diff-cover focuses the gate on new work.

rule.statement: Every public function with a `///` doc block must include at least one `# Examples` section with a runnable code block (no `ignore` or `no_run` unless side-effecting code is involved).
rule.rationale: Documentation with no example becomes outdated silently. Doctests are tests — when the example breaks, CI catches it.

rule.statement: Parsers, codecs, serializers, and data-structure invariants MUST have at least one `proptest!` block exercising the invariant. Roundtrip (`decode(encode(x)) == x`) is the canonical shape.
rule.rationale: Hand-written examples miss adversarial inputs. Proptest's shrinking finds minimal counter-examples automatically.

rule.statement: Test modules use `#[cfg(test)] mod tests { ... }` and are never `pub`. Test-only helpers go behind `#[cfg(test)]` or in a separate `dev-dependencies` test crate.

rationale.source: Rust Book ch.11; Cargo Book — dev-dependencies

rule.rationale: Test code in pub namespace bloats downstream consumers' compile time and exposes internals that are not API.

rule.statement: Integration tests live in `tests/` at crate root. Each file is a separate crate — shared helpers go in `tests/common/mod.rs` (subdir, not file, so Cargo does not treat it as a test target).
rule.rationale: Cargo compiles every top-level file in `tests/` as its own integration crate. A `tests/common.rs` (single-file) would compile + run as an empty test target.
